2603.21014.title: CLT-Forge: A Scalable Library for Cross-Layer Transcoders and Attribution Graphs
2603.21014.paper_summary: Mechanistic interpretability seeks to understand how Large Language Models (LLMs) represent and process information. Recent approaches based on dictionary learning and transcoders enable representing model computation in terms of sparse, interpretable features and their interactions, giving rise to feature attribution graphs. However, these graphs are often large and redundant, limiting their interpretability in practice. Cross-Layer Transcoders (CLTs) address this issue by sharing features across layers while preserving layer-specific decoding, yielding more compact representations, but remain difficult to train and analyze at scale. We introduce an open-source library for end-to-end training and interpretability of CLTs. Our framework integrates scalable distributed training with model sharding and compressed activation caching, a unified automated interpretability pipeline for feature analysis and explanation, attribution graph computation using Circuit-Tracer, and a flexible visualization interface. This provides a practical and unified solution for scaling CLT-based mechanistic interpretability. Our code is available at: https://github.com/LLM-Interp/CLT-Forge.
2603.21014.paper_summary_zh: 機械解釋性旨在理解大型語言模型（LLMs）如何表示和處理信息。<lb/>基於字典學習和轉碼器的最新方法使得能夠以稀疏、可解釋的特徵及其相互作用來表示模型計算，從而產生特徵歸因圖。<lb/>然而，這些圖通常龐大且冗餘，限制了它們在實踐中的可解釋性。<lb/>跨層轉碼器（CLTs）通過在保留層特定解碼的同時跨層共享特徵來解決這一問題，從而產生更緊湊的表示，但在大規模訓練和分析方面仍然困難。<lb/>我們介紹了一個開源庫，用於CLTs的端到端訓練和可解釋性。<lb/>我們的框架整合了可擴展的分佈式訓練、模型分片和壓縮激活緩存，提供了一個統一的自動化可解釋性管道，用於特徵分析和解釋、使用Circuit-Tracer的歸因圖計算，以及靈活的可視化界面。<lb/>這為基於CLT的機械解釋性提供了一個實用且統一的解決方案。<lb/>我們的代碼可在以下位置獲得：https://github.com/LLM-Interp/CLT-Forge。
2603.21014.author: Florent Draye et.al.
2603.21014.authors: Florent Draye, Abir Harrasse, Vedant Palit, Tung-Yu Wu, Jiarui Liu, Punya Syon Pandey, Roderick Wu, Terry Jingchen Zhang, Zhijing Jin, Bernhard Schölkopf
